_label_note: UK Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public HR assistance-line bait, UK entity/FRN/UTR numeric fragments, obfuscated employee/session IDs, system-bucket account bait, genetic-data metaphor wording, and public/manual employment-matter material-event bait. UK IDTA labels were kept as deterministic cross-border-transfer findings where the fixture describes actual payroll/HR data exports.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: has not been disclosed via RNS
An insider list has been created
Scope 2 figures for FY2025 require restatement

On 29 May, a targeted breach was detected in our London data centre affecting the beta environment of Prism Wallet (cryptoasset custody), with approximately 3% of test accounts experiencing unauthorised API calls; this has not been disclosed via RNS.

Separately, the Sustainability team advises that Scope 2 figures for FY2025 require restatement due to metering errors at the Hull facility, increasing reported emissions by 14%, which may affect eligibility under our green financing covenants.
The Board will consider whether an impairment charge is required for capitalised development costs of Prism Wallet if launch slips beyond September, which may affect guidance.
